message:
I've been thinking about heat waves lately—they're one of those emergencies that builds slowly, which actually means we have time to think things through ahead of time. I found a solid, straightforward resource that walks through the basics of staying safe and comfortable when temperatures spike, and honestly, it takes a lot of the guesswork out of it. The nice part is that most of the ideas are pretty simple and doable.

What's one heat-safety thing you've already got figured out, or what would be helpful to know more about?
